_meta.purpose: Acknowledgment and repair lexicons for R+ classification (Paper 2)
_meta.author: Torami x Boss | IMT Attention project | Paper 2 / R+ direct-coding | 2026-06-16
_meta.validation_status: Initial draft — requires native-speaker validation per language
changelog_v2: Repair detection no longer relies solely on lexicon substring match;
the v2 classifier requires the caregiver utterance to BE a clarification request
(short utterance ≤ 3 tokens) rather than merely CONTAIN a repair word.
This fixes the 'what is that' false-positive (11.5% of Brown contingent episodes).
The lexicon itself is unchanged from v1; only the matching logic in 10_extract_R_plus_v2.py changed.
notes: Repair: clarification requests; contingent but non-positive, kept separate from R+ depth
Repair v2 logic: caregiver utterance must be ≤ 3 tokens AND match a repair item
Items are matched case-insensitively with light punctuation stripping
CJK languages (Japanese, Mandarin) use character-based matching
Latin-script languages (English, Spanish) use word-boundary matching for acknowledgment
Repair uses utterance-level token-count gate (added in v2)
